curriculum.title: HTML and CSS Fundamentals
curriculum.description: Learn how to build and style webpages using HTML and CSS together. This course teaches structure, design, and layout step by step for young learners.

lessons.title: What Are HTML and CSS?
lessons.body: HTML builds the structure of a webpage while CSS controls how it looks.
lessons.avatar_script: HTML is like the bones of a webpage. CSS is like the clothes and colors. HTML tells the browser what things are, and CSS makes them beautiful.

questions.question: CSS controls how a webpage looks.
Create a paragraph with the text 'Hello world'.
questions.explanation: Great job! Paragraphs are the building blocks of text content on webpages. They help organize your writing into readable chunks.

code_example.explanation:
Let me show you how to create a paragraph. I use the 'p' tag - that stands for paragraph. I write the opening tag

, then my text 'Hello world', and close it with

. The text goes between the opening and closing tags. Every paragraph needs both tags to work properly!

lessons.title: HTML Review: Headings and Text
lessons.body: Headings show titles and paragraphs show text.
lessons.avatar_script: Headings help people know what a page is about. Paragraphs explain ideas using text.

questions.question: Paragraphs are used to show text.
Create an h2 heading with the text 'About Me'.
questions.explanation: Well done! Headings help organize your page into sections. The h2 tag is perfect for section titles, while h1 is usually for the main page title.

code_example.explanation:
Let me show you headings. HTML has six heading levels, from h1 (biggest) to h6 (smallest). I'm using h2 for a section title. I write

, then my text 'About Me', and close with

. Headings make your page easier to read and help people find what they're looking for!

lessons.title: What Is CSS?
lessons.body: CSS is used to add color, size, and layout to webpages.
lessons.avatar_script: CSS tells the browser how things should look. You can change colors, sizes, and spacing.

questions.question: Change paragraph text color to red.
questions.explanation: Excellent! You're learning how CSS styles HTML elements. The color property is one of the most useful CSS properties you'll use!

code_example.explanation: Now let's style with CSS! To change text color, I first write the selector 'p' to target paragraphs. Then I add curly brackets. Inside, I write 'color: red;' - 'color' is the property, and 'red' is the value. Don't forget the colon after the property and semicolon at the end!

lessons.title: Text Color and Size
lessons.body: CSS can change how text looks.
lessons.avatar_script: You can make text bigger, smaller, or colorful using CSS.

questions.question: Set text size to 20px.
questions.explanation: Nice work! Controlling text size helps make your content readable. Bigger text is easier to read, while smaller text fits more content.

code_example.explanation: Let me show you how to change text size. I use the 'font-size' property - notice the hyphen between 'font' and 'size'. I set it to '20px', which means 20 pixels. Pixels are tiny dots on your screen. The bigger the number, the bigger the text will be!

lessons.title: Background Colors
lessons.body: Background colors help sections stand out.
lessons.avatar_script: Background colors make parts of your page easy to see.

questions.question: Background colors can be applied to divs.
Set background color to yellow.
questions.explanation: Great! Background colors make your page sections pop and help organize content visually. Yellow is a bright, cheerful choice!

code_example.explanation: Let me show you background colors. I use 'background-color' as the property - notice the hyphen! I set it to 'yellow' to create a bright background. You can use any color name like yellow, pink, lightgreen, or even special codes for custom colors!

lessons.title: Styling Headings
lessons.body: Headings can be styled to look bold and clear.
lessons.avatar_script: Big colorful headings help people read your page easily.

questions.question: Headings can be styled using CSS.
Change heading color to blue.
questions.explanation: Well done! Colorful headings catch the reader's eye and make your page more interesting. Blue is a popular choice for professional-looking pages!

code_example.explanation: Let me style a heading. I use 'h1' as my selector to target the main heading. Then I set 'color: blue;' to make the text blue. This same technique works for h2, h3, and other heading levels too. You can make each heading level a different color if you want!

lessons.title: The Box Model
lessons.body: Every HTML element is a box.
lessons.avatar_script: Each box has content, padding, border, and margin.

questions.question: Add 20px padding.
questions.explanation: Perfect! Padding creates breathing room inside elements. It keeps your content from touching the edges, making everything look cleaner.

code_example.explanation: Let me explain padding. Padding adds space INSIDE an element, between the content and its border. Think of it like the cushioning inside a picture frame. I write 'padding: 20px;' to add 20 pixels of space on all four sides. This makes the content feel less cramped!

lessons.title: Margin and Padding
lessons.avatar_script: Margins push elements apart. Padding gives space inside.

questions.question: Set margin to 30px.
questions.explanation: Excellent! Margin creates space OUTSIDE elements, keeping them from crowding each other. This helps your page look organized and professional.

code_example.explanation: Now let's learn margin. Unlike padding which is inside, margin adds space OUTSIDE an element. It's like personal space between people standing in line! I write 'margin: 30px;' to add 30 pixels of space around the element. This keeps it separated from everything else on the page.

lessons.title: Width and Height
lessons.avatar_script: You can control how big things appear.

questions.question: Height controls how tall an element is.
Set height to 150px.
questions.explanation: Great job! The height property lets you control exactly how tall an element should be. This is useful for creating consistent layouts.

code_example.explanation: Let me show you how to set height. The 'height' property controls how tall an element is. I write 'height: 150px;' to make it exactly 150 pixels tall. You can also use 'width' to control how wide it is. Together, these properties let you create boxes of any size you want!

lessons.title: Combining HTML and CSS
lessons.body: HTML builds pages and CSS styles them.
lessons.avatar_script: When HTML and CSS work together, your page looks amazing.

questions.question: Change text color to green.
questions.explanation: Wonderful! You're combining HTML and CSS like a real web developer. Green is a great color that's easy on the eyes!

code_example.explanation: Let me change the text to green. I select the element I want to style, then use 'color: green;' inside the curly brackets. CSS knows many color names - you can try green, darkgreen, lightgreen, or even lime for different shades of green!

lessons.title: Styling a Card
lessons.avatar_script: Cards are used everywhere to group content.

questions.question: Add a 2px solid border.
questions.explanation: Excellent! Borders frame your content and make cards look polished. You've learned how to combine thickness, style, and color in one property!

code_example.explanation: Let me show you borders. The border property needs three values: thickness, style, and color. I write 'border: 2px solid black;' - that's 2 pixels thick, a solid line (not dotted or dashed), and black color. All three go together with spaces between them. This creates a nice frame around the element!

lessons.title: My First Styled Page
lessons.body: Build and style your own page.
lessons.avatar_script: You are now a webpage designer!

questions.question: Set paragraph font size to 20px.
questions.explanation: Congratulations! You've completed the course and learned how to build and style webpages. You're now a webpage designer!

code_example.explanation: For our final lesson, let's style paragraphs. I use 'font-size: 20px;' to make the text a nice readable size. Remember, you can combine multiple properties together - like adding color too! You've learned so much about HTML and CSS. Now you can create your own beautiful webpages!
